validated_commands.build_note: Full workspace build requires goose git dep (network blocked in CI); persistence crate builds/tests independently

open_prs.title: [Perf Improver] INSERT RETURNING in schedule/trigger/plugin create (−1 SQL round-trip each)
open_prs.note: PR number pending - submitted via safeoutputs

merged_prs.title: [Perf Improver] SQLite PRAGMA tuning: ~12x write throughput improvement in WAL mode
[Perf Improver] Add Criterion benchmarks to opengoose-persistence
[Perf Improver] Atomic append_message: single WAL commit per message write
[Perf Improver] SQLite mmap_size PRAGMA: memory-mapped reads (128 MiB)

optimization_backlog.title: INSERT RETURNING in schedule/trigger/plugin create
optimization_backlog.notes: Replaces INSERT+SELECT with INSERT RETURNING in ScheduleStore::create, TriggerStore::create, PluginStore::install. Saves 1 round-trip per create. RETURNING feature already in workspace deps.

optimization_backlog.title: Cache recent session history in memory
optimization_backlog.notes: load_history() hits SQLite on every Goose agent invocation. An LRU cache per session would avoid repeated reads. Requires more significant code change and thread-safety considerations. dashmap already in workspace deps.

perf_notes: Full workspace build downloads goose from GitHub (network blocked). Use -p opengoose-persistence for local work.
SQLite synchronous=NORMAL in WAL mode is safe and ~12x faster for per-commit writes. PR #39 merged.
libsqlite3-sys with bundled feature - SQLite version is bundled, no system SQLite dependency.
Criterion benchmarks baselines (in-memory WAL): append_message ~15-16µs, get_active_team ~1µs, load_history(10) ~6µs, load_history(50) ~28µs, set_active_team ~9µs.
In-memory SQLite doesn't show mmap benefit - file-based workloads gain from mmap_size on repeated page reads.
conn.transaction() requires PersistenceError: From<diesel::result::Error> which is already implemented.
returning_clauses_for_sqlite_3_35 feature already in workspace - INSERT RETURNING works and is used in MessageQueue::enqueue.
schedule_store bench has a pre-existing UniqueViolation bug (creates same-named schedule twice without cleanup) - unrelated to our changes.
All four previous PRs merged quickly by maintainer.
